feed.subtitle: A minimal way to show off something which I work on.

entry.title: CMake First Project
entry.summary: Creating Your First CMake Project Project Structure hello/ ├── CMakeLists.txt └── main.cpp Writing the Source Code main.cpp #include <iostream> int main() { std::cout << "Hello CMake Build!" << std::endl; return 0; } Creating the CMakeLists.txt File touch CMakeLists.txt CMakeLists.txt cmake_minimum_required(VERSION 3.10) project(Hello) add_executable(he...

entry.title: Process vs Threads
entry.summary: Linux Internals — Process vs Thread Process Definition A process is an independent running program with its own memory and resources. Features Separate address space Own PID Own heap, stack, globals Heavyweight Creation fork(); Example firefox vim gcc Thread Definition A thread is a lightweight execution unit inside a process. Features Shares process memory Own...

entry.title: Understanding ARM Registers
entry.summary: Introduction to ARM Registers Registers hold the data and instructions that the processor is currently working on. Understanding how the ARM architecture utilizes registers is important for ARM. The ARM architecture is a Load-Store architecture. This means data must be moved from memory into a register before it can be processed. Once processed, the result is stored back into memory from the r...

entry.title: ARM Architecture Overview
entry.summary: Architecture Overview Modern computing is largely shaped by two dominant processor architectures: ARM (RISC) and x86 (CISC). While both are designed to execute instructions and run applications, they follow fundamentally different design philosophies. ARM (Reduced Instruction Set Computing) focuses on simplicity and efficiency. It uses a smaller, fixed set of instructions that can be executed...

entry.title: CMake Beginner Guide
entry.summary: Getting Started with CMake Introduction When building C or C++ projects, managing source files, dependencies, compiler options, and cross-platform compatibility can quickly become difficult. This is where CMake becomes essential. CMake is an open-source build system generator that helps developers automate the compilation process across different platforms and compilers. Instead of writing s...
